common_nigerian_presentation: I am feeling hot with severe body pain and bitter taste in my mouth.
recommended_action: Perform malaria RDT, assess hydration, give ACT if positive per national guideline, and provide return precautions.

common_nigerian_presentation: This fever has stayed for days and my stomach is turning me.
recommended_action: Assess for prolonged fever causes, request appropriate diagnostics, avoid empiric over-treatment, and monitor for danger signs.

common_nigerian_presentation: My head is heavy and I feel like my pressure is high.
recommended_action: Check blood pressure twice, counsel on salt reduction and medication adherence, and refer urgently if severe symptoms present.

common_nigerian_presentation: My chest and upper stomach are burning after I eat.
recommended_action: Screen for alarm symptoms, advise dietary modification, consider antacid/PPI trial, and schedule follow-up review.

common_nigerian_presentation: I have catarrh, cough, and throat pain since yesterday.
recommended_action: Assess breathing status, provide symptomatic care, encourage fluids, and educate on warning signs requiring reassessment.

common_nigerian_presentation: Urine is peppering me and I keep going to toilet often.
recommended_action: Take focused history, perform urinalysis where available, treat per protocol, and refer if fever/flank pain or pregnancy.

common_nigerian_presentation: The child is becoming too slim and not eating well.
recommended_action: Screen with MUAC and oedema check, start nutrition support protocol, and refer severe acute malnutrition immediately.

common_nigerian_presentation: I am pregnant and seeing blood with serious headache and swollen legs.
recommended_action: Treat as emergency danger sign, stabilize per protocol, and arrange urgent referral to higher-level obstetric care.

common_nigerian_presentation: I have a painful boil with pus on my leg.
recommended_action: Examine lesion extent, clean wound, provide indicated antibiotics, and refer if spreading infection or systemic signs.

common_nigerian_presentation: My child is stooling water many times and becoming weak.
recommended_action: Assess dehydration immediately, begin ORS and zinc, continue feeding, and escalate if severe dehydration or blood in stool.
